title: Cannot start podman due to missing image
body:
### Issue Description When I run `limactl start podman` I get the following output: ``` INFO[0000] Using the existing instance "podman" INFO[0000] Starting the instance "podman" with VM driver "qemu" INFO[0000] QEMU binary "/usr/local/bin/qemu-system-x86_64" seems properly signed with the "com.apple.security.hypervisor" entitlement INFO[0000] Attempting to download the image arch=x86_64 digest= location="https://cloud-images.ubuntu.com/impish/current/impish-server-cloudimg-amd64.img" FATA[0000] failed to download "https://cloud-images.ubuntu.com/impish/current/impish-server-cloudimg-amd64.img": unexpected HTTP status Not Found, body="\n\n404 Not Found\n\n

Not Found

\n

The requested URL was not found on this server.

\n
\n
Apache/2.4.29 (Ubuntu) Server at cloud-images.ubuntu.com Port 443
\n\n" ``` I am on macOS Ventura 13.2.1, limactl version 0.20.2 ### Steps to reproduce the issue Steps to reproduce the issue 1. Run `limactl start podman` ### Describe the results you received ``` NFO[0000] Using the existing instance "podman" INFO[0000] Starting the instance "podman" with VM driver "qemu" INFO[0000] QEMU binary "/usr/local/bin/qemu-system-x86_64" seems properly signed with the "com.apple.security.hypervisor" entitlement INFO[0000] Attempting to download the image arch=x86_64 digest= location="https://cloud-images.ubuntu.com/impish/current/impish-server-cloudimg-amd64.img" FATA[0000] failed to download "https://cloud-images.ubuntu.com/impish/current/impish-server-cloudimg-amd64.img": unexpected HTTP status Not Found, body="\n\n404 Not Found\n\n

Not Found

\n

The requested URL was not found on this server.

\n
\n
Apache/2.4.29 (Ubuntu) Server at cloud-images.ubuntu.com Port 443
\n\n" ``` ### Describe the results you expected I expected podman to start ### podman info output I can't run podman ### Podman in a container No ### Privileged Or Rootless None ### Upstream Latest Release Yes

comments.body:
You will probably need to `limactl edit podman`, and upgrade your template

https://github.com/lima-vm/lima/commit/e43ab22ae9755e4962e3f01838d6aef774c9ff62
This is not a podman issue

It is listed in the Podman Desktop docs (i.e. how to run Podman with Lima), but it doesn't describe how to update older instances - only how to create a new one from scratch... (which is one option I suppose)

https://podman-desktop.io/docs/lima/creating-a-lima-instance
